weather.summary: Saturday: 14°C, overcast clouds. Sunday: 16°C, broken clouds

metadata.greeting: Get ready for a fun-filled weekend in London! I've found some amazing activities for you and your 3-year-old, from exploring city farms to enjoying interactive theater shows and exciting museum events. With the weather looking good, it's a perfect time to get out and explore!

activities.title: Tiny Planet
activities.description: Theatre show for children
activities.whyItWorks: Good for 5+, but engaging visuals and sounds can still capture a 3-year-old's attention.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR from Hackney to Limehouse.

activities.title: Haringey Huskies vs Chelmsford Warriors
activities.description: Ice hockey match
activities.whyItWorks: Exciting and fast-paced, good for short bursts of attention.

transport.fromHackney: Take the W3 bus towards Finsbury Park, then take the Picadilly Line to Alexandra Palace

activities.title: Spitalfields City Farm
activities.description: Community city farm with animals and gardens.
activities.whyItWorks: Animals, outdoor space, and a relaxed atmosphere.

transport.fromHackney: Take the 26, 48, or 55 bus towards Shoreditch High Street.

activities.title: Quentin Blake: The Southbank Parade
activities.description: Exhibition of Quentin Blake's illustrations.
activities.whyItWorks: Engaging visuals and accessible art.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then the tube to Waterloo.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Road, Bethnal Green, London E2 9PA
activities.description: Museum of childhood with toys, design and interactive exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: Sensory play, soft spaces, and toddler-specific activities.

transport.fromHackney: Take bus 388 towards London Bridge and get off at Bethnal Green Station.

activities.title: Last Unicorn Airways
activities.description: An immersive installation theatre for kids
activities.whyItWorks: Sensory experience that can capture a 3-year-old's attention.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR from Hackney to Limehouse.

activities.title: Mudchute Park and Farm
activities.address: Pier Street, Isle of Dogs, London E14 3HP
activities.description: One of the largest city farms, with free entry and lots of animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of space to run around and see farm animals.

activities.title: Gozzle at Walthamstow Wetlands
activities.description: Interactive trail to discover what it takes for Gozzle to become a grown-up goose.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive trail suitable for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Bus to Tottenham Hale, then short walk

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaurs, animals, and interactive exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaurs, large animal exhibits, and interactive displays appeal to young children.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Piccadilly Line to South Kensington
transport.tip: Visit the dinosaurs early to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.address: Exhibition Road, South Kensington, London SW7 2DD
activities.description: Interactive exhibits on science and technology.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on exhibits that stimulate curiosity and learning.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Piccadilly Line to South Kensington.

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.address: 100 London Road, Forest Hill, London SE23 3PQ
activities.description: Natural history, musical instruments, and gardens.
activities.whyItWorks: Animals, gardens, and hands-on exhibits.

activities.title: London Zoo's Zoonormous Egg Hunt
activities.description: Easter egg hunt and animal encounters.
activities.whyItWorks: Animals and egg hunt create an exciting experience.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Regent's Park or Camden Town, then walk

activities.title: Kew Gardens – Octonauts Above and Beyond
activities.description: An interactive trail that invites young explorers to complete challenges, discover facts and learn how to protect our planet.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive trail suitable for young children, inspired by a popular series.

activities.title: Battersea Park
activities.description: Large park with playground, zoo and picnic areas.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of space to run around, playground and animals.

transport.fromHackney: Train to Clapham Junction, then bus

activities.title: Cutty Sark Easter Egg Trail
activities.address: King William Walk, Greenwich, London SE10 9HT
activities.description: Easter egg trail throughout the historic ship, discover facts about Cutty Sark's voyages.
activities.whyItWorks: Exploration and Easter egg hunting.

activities.title: Postal Museum
activities.description: Interactive exhibits celebrating the history of the postal service.
activities.whyItWorks: Mail Rail ride and interactive exhibits.

activities.title: The Very Hungry Caterpillar Show
activities.description: An adaptation of four of Eric Carle's books. Brought to life by more than 75 puppets.
activities.whyItWorks: Puppets and familiar stories

activities.title: I Want My Hat Back Trilogy
activities.description: Jon Klassen's beloved books, I Want My Hat Back, This is Not My Hat and We Found A Hat, have found a new life of their own LIVE on stage.
activities.whyItWorks: Good for ages 3-6. All your favourites including Bear, Little Fish and the Turtles will be making an appearance in this one-of-a-kind trilogy.

activities.title: British Library's Fairy Tales Exhibition
activities.description: Explore enchanted lands, magical creatures, iconic characters and timeless tales, brought to life through books, theatre costumes, puppets, pop-ups, artwork and illustrations.
activities.whyItWorks: Brings books to life.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Explore the gardens in search of the Lindt Gold Bunny statues peeking out throughout the palace grounds for a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor energy-burner and chocolate treat.

activities.title: The Flying Bath
activities.description: When everyone has left the house in the morning, the bath toys spring into action and head off on exciting adventures in their magical flying bath!
activities.whyItWorks: Good for ages 2-5.

activities.title: Disney's Hercules
activities.description: A wise-cracking, column-shaking, underworld-rocking musical ride of adventure and self-discovery.
activities.whyItWorks: An all-ages musical

activities.title: London Transport Museum - Future London
activities.description: Take part in fun family activities, creating your own visions of London in 2126.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on craft activities, design the skyline, meet time-travelling artist.

transport.tip: Check details on the LTM website closer to the time.

activities.title: Peppa Pig's egg-stra special Easter Afternoon Tea Bus Tour
activities.description: Peppa Pig themed afternoon tea will be run with special Easter travel cup and Easter Egg hunt activity on a London bus.
activities.whyItWorks: Suitable for children ages 2+.

transport.tip: Check the website to confirm the route.
